FCM service used in manifest."}"}}]},{"role":"tool","name":"read_file","tool_call_id":"call_5JaXw0h1uUgR5pj0xvRh7fwY","content":"package com.rio.rostry.service

import android.app.NotificationChannel
import android.app.NotificationManager
import android.content.Context
import android.os.Build
import androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat
import com.google.firebase.messaging.FirebaseMessagingService
import com.google.firebase.messaging.RemoteMessage
import com.rio.rostry.R
import com.rio.rostry.data.repo.UserRepository
import dagger.hilt.android.AndroidEntryPoint
import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
import kotlinx.coroutines.SupervisorJob
import kotlinx.coroutines.cancel
import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
import timber.log.Timber
import javax.inject.Inject

@AndroidEntryPoint
class RostryMessagingService : FirebaseMessagingService() {

 @Inject
 lateinit var userRepository: UserRepository

 private val serviceScope = CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.IO)

 override fun onNewToken(token: String) {
 super.onNewToken(token)
 Timber.d("FCM Token: %s", token)
 serviceScope.launch {
 userRepository.updateFcmToken(token)
 }
 }

 override fun onMessageReceived(remoteMessage: RemoteMessage) {
 super.onMessageReceived(remoteMessage)
 Timber.d("From: %s", remoteMessage.from)

 // Check if message contains a data payload.
 remoteMessage.data.isNotEmpty().let {
 Timber.d("Message data payload: " + remoteMessage.data)
 // Handle data payload
 }

 // Check if message contains a notification payload.
 remoteMessage.notification?.let {
 Timber.d("Message Notification Body: %s", it.body)
 sendNotification(it.title, it.body)
 }
 }

 private fun sendNotification(title: String?, messageBody: String?) {
 val channelId = "default_channel_id"
 val notificationBuilder = NotificationCompat.Builder(this, channelId)
 .setSmallIcon(R.drawable.ic_launcher_foreground) // Using a default icon
 .setContentTitle(title)
 .setContentText(messageBody)
 .setAutoCancel(true)

 val notificationManager = getSystemService(Context.NOTIFICATION_SERVICE) as NotificationManager

 if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
 val channel = NotificationChannel(
 channelId,
 "Default Channel",
 NotificationManager.IMPORTANCE_DEFAULT
 )
 notificationManager.createNotificationChannel(channel)
 }

 notificationManager.notify(0, notificationBuilder.build())
 }

 override fun onDestroy() {
 super.onDestroy()
 serviceScope.cancel()
 }
}

High-level overview
- **Platform**: Android app (Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, Hilt, Room, WorkManager) + Firebase backend (Auth, Firestore, Storage, Messaging, Crashlytics) + Cloud Functions (Node 20).
- **Architecture**: MVVM with repositories as single source of truth; DI via Hilt; offline-first with Room cache and background sync; Compose Navigation.
- **Security**: Strict Firestore rules; sensitive operations (fowl transfer) go through Cloud Functions; fraud detection and admin analytics in backend.
- **Modules**: Single Android module `app` and a `functions` folder for Firebase Functions.

### Code structure
- Android entry points:
 - `app/src/main/java/com/rio/rostry/MainActivity.kt`
 - `app/src/main/java/com/rio/rostry/RostryApplication.kt` (Hilt + WorkManager + prefetch)
 - `app/src/main/java/com/rio/rostry/navigation/NavGraph.kt` (screen routing)
- Data layer:
 - Room DB: `RostryDatabase.kt`, DAOs under `data/local`
 - Models under `data/models`
 - Repositories under `data/repo` with offline-first strategies
- UI: Compose screens in `ui/...` (auth, fowl, market, messaging, transfer, analytics)
- DI: Hilt modules in `di/*`
- Utils: network monitoring, performance logging, prefetch, QR, CSV
- Notifications: `service/RostryMessagingService.kt` (FCM)
- Backend:
 - `functions/index.js` orchestrates callable/triggers
 - Fraud detection, admin dashboard, analytics, market insights utilities
 - Firestore rules, indexes, Firebase config at repo root

### Build and dependencies
- Versions managed in `gradle/libs.versions.toml`
- App config in `app/build.gradle.kts` (Compose, Hilt, Room, Paging, Coroutines, Firebase, Stripe, WorkManager)
- Plugins in root `build.gradle.kts` and `settings.gradle.kts` with `:app` only

### Data flow (key features)
- Auth/profile: Firebase Auth; profile in `users/{uid}`; FCM token saved via `UserRepository`.
- Fowls: Local-first CRUD via Room; Firestore sync in background; paging for lists.
- Transfers:
 - Client initiates through callable `initiateTransfer`
 - Receiver approves via callable `approveTransfer` (server sets COMPLETED)
 - Status changes trigger `onTransferStatusChange` to update ownership and send FCM
 - Firestore Rules restrict who can read/write and which transitions are allowed
- Messaging: Conversations and messages with participant-based access rules.
- Analytics: Client queues events (DataStore) and sends batches to Functions; backend provides farm trends/admin stats.
- Sync: Periodic `SyncWorker` runs hourly via WorkManager.

### Security highlights
- Firestore rules enforce:
 - Users only read/write their own docs
 - Transfer state transitions constrained; COMPLETED set server-side only
 - Admin collections locked to Functions
- Cloud Functions add:
 - Auth checks, rate limiting, duplicate prevention
 - Ownership verification, encryption helpers
 - Fraud detection (real-time and scheduled) + admin alerts
- Crashlytics and logging present

### Offline-first and performance
- Room-first writes with background Firestore sync
- Network-aware operations via `NetworkMonitor`
- `PerformanceLogger` records request timings and success
- Prefetch on app start for faster UX in rural/low-connectivity

### Internationalization and low-end devices
- Current `strings.xml` only has `app_name`. No Telugu resources yet. Add `app/src/main/res/values-te/strings.xml` for Telugu.
- Compose UI + paging + local cache are friendly to low-end devices; continue optimizing image sizes (Coil), avoid heavy recompositions, and batch network calls.

### Where to start reading
- Application setup:
```12:14:app/src/main/java/com/rio/rostry/RostryApplication.kt
@HiltAndroidApp
class RostryApplication : Application(), Configuration.Provider {
 // Timber, WorkManager hourly sync, prefetch on startup
}
```
- Navigation and screens:
```12:14:app/src/main/java/com/rio/rostry/navigation/NavGraph.kt
NavHost(navController = navController, startDestination = "auth_graph") {
 // auth_graph -> main_graph (lists, details, transfers, settings, analytics)
}
```
- Transfer call to backend:
```12:14:app/src/main/java/com/rio/rostry/data/repo/TransferRepositoryImpl.kt
val result = functions.getHttpsCallable("initiateTransfer").call(data).await()
```
- Cloud Functions surface:
```12:14:functions/index.js
exports.initiateTransfer = functions.https.onCall(async (data, context) => { /* auth, rate limit, validate, encrypt, write */ });
exports.approveTransfer = functions.https.onCall(/* ... */);
exports.rejectTransfer = functions.https.onCall(/* ... */);
exports.onTransferStatusChange = functions.firestore.document("transfers/{id}").onUpdate(/* ownership + FCM */);
```

### Run it locally
- Android app:
```bash
./gradlew clean assembleDebug
./gradlew :app:installDebug
```
- Unit/UI tests:
```bash
./gradlew test
./gradlew connectedAndroidTest
```
- Backend (from repo root):
```bash
cd functions && npm ci
firebase emulators:start --only functions,firestore
# deploy (when ready)
firebase deploy --only functions
```

### Gaps and priorities
- Telugu localization not present; add `values-te` resources.
- Confirm all Room entities/DAOs have tests and cover offline/merge conflict edges.
- Ensure transfer completion in client relies on server path (it already calls approveTransfer; avoid client writing COMPLETED to Firestore directly).
- Validate crash reporting and analytics sampling in release.
- Check WorkManager constraints for metered/low battery for rural scenarios.

- Build config and compilation look consistent; Gradle uses Kotlin DSL and version catalogs; Hilt and KSP configured correctly.

- If you want, I can generate a minimal onboarding README and add Telugu `values-te/strings.xml` to kickstart localization.

- I scanned the project structure, build files, manifest, DI modules, repositories, UI navigation, Firebase rules, and Cloud Functions to map the architecture and data/security flows."}]},{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"<user_query>
build and deploy the project and take eye on functions and backend !
